context: Evaluate whether the agent applies the skill's structured four-part response format correctly for a multi-issue script

checklist.name: Four-part format applied to every issue
checklist.description: For each issue the agent reports, the response includes all four parts: (a) problematic code snippet, (b) issue explanation with ShellCheck code where applicable, (c) corrected code, (d) explanation of why the fix is better

checklist.name: Unquoted variables identified
checklist.description: Agent identifies unquoted $CONFIG in grep, cut subshells, and the [ ] comparison; unquoted $LOG_FILE in the redirect; and unquoted $key/$value in export

checklist.name: == in [ ] identified
checklist.description: Agent identifies that [ $CONFIG == "" ] uses == which is not POSIX-compliant in [ ]; suggests = instead, and quotes the variable

checklist.name: Useless use of cat or piping anti-pattern flagged
checklist.description: Agent identifies that grep ... | while read line with unquoted $line is unsafe; suggests IFS= read -r line and quoting; alternatively flags the useless-use-of-cat/pipe pattern if grep -r is unnecessary

checklist.name: export key=value injection risk flagged
checklist.description: Agent identifies that export $key=$value can overwrite critical environment variables if CONFIG is attacker-controlled and recommends validation or a safer approach

checklist.name: Summary section with counts and verdict
checklist.description: Response ends with a summary section listing error count, warning count, info count, and a clear PASSED or FAILED verdict
